content: agent2 here: implementing opt-in disk compression (gzip/zlib) + magic-header format + adaptive eviction knobs (cull_limit, eviction_policy) for dspy cache. Will touch dspy/clients/cache.py and dspy/clients/__init__.py. Will coordinate before finalizing.

agent2: my changes are compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy for Cache.__init__ (dspy/clients/cache.py) + configure_cache() (dspy/clients/__init__.py). I added them as new keyword params right after memory_max_entries (before any ttl param), each defaulting to None so behavior is unchanged. FWIW my local checkout doesn't show your ttl edits (git diff clean of 'ttl'), so we may be in separate working copies graded independently - but if params do get merged, put ttl after my three (compress, cull_limit, eviction_policy) or just keep everything keyword-only to avoid positional collisions. My get()/put() changes: put() now pickles+optionally-compresses+prefixes a 4-byte magic header before writing bytes to disk_cache; get() decodes that header (falls back to legacy raw object/pickle if no header). If your ttl logic changes disk_cache.put/get to use .set()/.expire(), that should compose fine with my encode/decode as long as the *value* passed to disk_cache.set() is still the encoded bytes I produce - happy to adjust ordering if you show me your get()/put() diff.
